fields.title: Getting Started with Django
fields.summary: Django is a powerful Python web framework that helps developers build web applications quickly and securely. It comes with many built-in features, making development faster and easier. By using models, views, and templates, Django keeps code organized and maintainable, making it a great choice for both beginners and experienced developers.
fields.content:
Django is a high-level Python web framework that allows developers to build secure and scalable web applications quickly. It follows the "batteries-included" philosophy, meaning it provides most of the tools you need out of the box.

Why Choose Django?

  • Fast development with built-in features
  • Secure by default
  • Scalable for large applications
  • Clean and maintainable code structure

Basic Structure

A Django project is made up of apps. Each app handles a specific part of the functionality. You define models for your data, views for logic, and templates for rendering HTML.

Simple Example

            
# views.py
from django.http import HttpResponse

def home(request):
    return HttpResponse("Hello, Django!")

This simple view returns a basic response. In real applications, you would use templates to render dynamic HTML content.

Conclusion

Django is a powerful framework for both beginners and experienced developers. With its simplicity and flexibility, it is a great choice for building modern web applications.

fields.title: Async JavaScript Basics
fields.summary: Learn how JavaScript handles slow tasks like API calls using callbacks, promises, and async/await.
fields.content:
Introduction

JavaScript runs one task at a time, but sometimes tasks take longer, like fetching data. Async programming helps handle these tasks without stopping the app.

What is Asynchronous Programming?

It allows JavaScript to continue running other code while waiting for a task to finish.

Callbacks

function fetchData(callback) {
    setTimeout(() => {
        callback("Data received");
    }, 1000);
}

Promises

function fetchData() {
    return new Promise((resolve) => {
        setTimeout(() => {
            resolve("Data received");
        }, 1000);
    });
}

Async/Await

async function getData() {
    const data = await fetchData();
    console.log(data);
}

Conclusion

Async programming makes your app faster and smoother.

fields.title: REST API Basics
fields.summary: Learn what REST APIs are and how clients communicate with servers using simple HTTP methods.
fields.content:
Introduction

REST APIs are used to connect frontend and backend systems. They allow applications to communicate over HTTP.

What is a REST API?

A REST API is a way for two systems to communicate using standard HTTP methods like GET, POST, PUT, and DELETE.

HTTP Methods

  • GET: Fetch data from server
  • POST: Send new data to server
  • PUT: Update existing data
  • DELETE: Remove data

Simple Example

fetch("https://api.example.com/users")
    .then(response => response.json())
    .then(data => console.log(data))
    .catch(error => console.error(error));

Why REST APIs are Important

  • Easy communication between systems
  • Used in almost every web app
  • Works with any frontend framework

Conclusion

REST APIs are the backbone of modern web applications. Understanding them is essential for every developer.

fields.title: Git Basics
fields.summary: Learn the basic Git commands to track changes, save versions, and collaborate on code.
fields.content:
Introduction

Git is a version control system used to track changes in code. It helps developers work together and manage project history.

What is Git?

Git saves snapshots of your project so you can go back to any previous version if needed.

Basic Commands

  • git init: Start a new Git repository
  • git add: Add files to staging area
  • git commit: Save changes
  • git status: Check current changes
  • git push: Send code to remote repository

Simple Workflow

git init
git add .
git commit -m "first commit"
git push origin main

Why Git is Important

  • Keeps track of code history
  • Helps team collaboration
  • Prevents losing work

Conclusion

Git is an essential tool for every developer. Learning it makes teamwork and coding much easier.
